expected_behavior: Must read references/collectors.md. Approach A (has @nestjs/config). Offer nest-profiler-mikro-orm and flag the ESM-only gotcha: it is ESM-only and must be imported AFTER MikroOrmModule; register MikroOrmCollectorModule.forRoot({ slowQueryThreshold: 100 }) — no dataSource needed (it wraps the ORM logger).

query:
package.json includes @nestjs/config, @nestjs/axios and axios.

User: I want to see the outgoing HTTP calls my app makes.
expected_behavior: Must read references/collectors.md. Offer nest-profiler-http and apply the 'bring your own client' gotcha: HttpCollectorModule.forRoot() is a no-op unless HttpModule (@nestjs/axios) is imported in the SAME module. Do not present it as automatic without the HttpModule import.

query:
package.json includes @nestjs/config, class-validator and class-transformer. main.ts already contains app.useGlobalPipes(new ValidationPipe({ whitelist: true })).

User: Add DTO validation profiling.
expected_behavior: Must read references/collectors.md. Offer nest-profiler-validator at the ROOT (global APP_PIPE). WARN that the existing separate global ValidationPipe must be removed — ProfilerValidationPipe already wraps a validation pipe (only one global validator). Fold its options into ValidatorCollectorModule.forRoot({ validationPipeOptions: { whitelist: true, transform: true } }). Note DTOs need value imports (not 'import type').

query:
package.json includes @nestjs/config and nest-commander. The app has both an HTTP server (main.ts) and a CLI entrypoint (cli.ts using CommandFactory.run).

User: I want to profile my CLI commands and see them in the profiler UI.
expected_behavior: Must read references/collectors.md. Offer nest-profiler-commander and apply the cross-process gotcha: CLI and HTTP are separate processes, so set storageType: 'file' on the core module; register CommanderCollectorModule.forRoot() in the module bootstrapped by CommandFactory.run AND import it in the HTTP app. Recommend adding .profiler/ to .gitignore.

query:
package.json includes @nestjs/config, @nestjs/graphql, @nestjs/apollo and graphql.

User: Add GraphQL profiling.
expected_behavior: Must read references/collectors.md. Offer nest-profiler-graphql with ProfilerGraphQLModule.forRoot() alongside GraphQLModule. Apply the gotcha: the GraphQLModule context MUST expose the request — for Apollo, context: ({ req }) => ({ req }). May suggest composing the core ignoreRequest with combineFilters(ignoreGraphQLPlayground, ignoreGraphQLIntrospection).

query: Why does the TypeORM collector use forRootAsync instead of forRoot?
expected_behavior: Must read references/collectors.md. Answer: because the DataSource is not available at module declaration time — register TypeOrmCollectorModule.forRootAsync({ inject: [DataSource], useFactory: (dataSource) => ({ dataSource, slowQueryThreshold: 100 }) }), placed after TypeOrmModule.

query: Should the config and validator collectors go in the root module or a feature module?
expected_behavior: Must read references/collectors.md. Answer: at the root — they install global providers (APP_PIPE for validator, a bootstrap snapshot for config). Infra-scoped collectors (database, http, cache, rabbitmq, graphql transport) stay co-located in the feature module that owns their infrastructure.
